title: feat: add db driver source evidence safe summary
goal: Prepare DB driver source evidence safe-summary contract for v1.1.8 without selecting a driver, adding a DB driver dependency, changing package.json or pnpm-lock, connecting to a real DB, executing migrations, running live DB integration tests, applying provider SDK actions, or claiming runtime/production/legal/YouTube policy readiness.

forbiddenScope: selected DB driver in committed evidence
approved final gate in committed evidence
DB driver selected in current PR

rollbackOrStopCondition: Stop if this PR adds a DB driver, real DB connection, package or lockfile change, migration execution, provider SDK apply, production deployment, readiness claim, secret value, or quality-gate weakening.
forbiddenScope: selected DB driver in committed evidence
approved final gate in committed evidence
DB driver selected in current PR
doneCriteria: candidate drivers remain exactly pg and postgres
future fresh fixture exists only in tests
no package or lockfile change is introduced
securityBoundaries: No DB driver dependency is added.
No package.json or pnpm-lock change is introduced.
No real DB connection is implemented.
No migration is changed or executed.
No live DB integration test is executed.
No provider SDK apply or production deployment is introduced.
No runtime, production, legal, or YouTube policy readiness is claimed.

Raw advisory output, raw audit output, raw OSV response, raw npm registry metadata, raw dependency trees, terminal output, private URLs, DB connection strings, wallet addresses, and token-like values are rejected.
residualRisks: The staleness policy is not an advisory review.
Future source evidence can still become stale if not rebound.
Future package version must match the dependency PR.
Future owner approval can expire or mismatch target.
Runtime and production readiness remain out of scope.
humanConfirmation: AI review is not human approval.
Future source evidence requires safe source review.
Current PR does not review or select a driver.
Future DB driver dependency introduction requires a separate project-owner-approved PR.

packageVerification.verification: No package scripts, runtime dependencies, DB driver dependency, package.json, or pnpm-lock changes are introduced by this PR.

apiCompatibilitySummary.compatibilityStatement: No runtime DB API or public API is changed; this adds an offline DB driver source evidence safe-summary validator and tests.

runtimeSmokeRationale: No runtime readiness is claimed; this PR changes offline DB driver source evidence safe-summary contract evidence and tests only.

reviewScope.scope: DB driver source evidence safe-summary validator, safe-summary schema, allowed counts/statuses, forbidden raw fields, forbidden wording, future reviewed fixture isolation, committed not-reviewed evidence, and tests.
reviewScope.riskSummary: Main risk is mistaking safe-summary contract readiness for advisory review; committed evidence remains not-reviewed, no-driver, and no-package-change.
reviewScope.verificationOracle: Vitest safe-summary coverage, typecheck, lint, evidence checks, quality self-protection, secret scan, and no-scraping scan.

reviewIndependence.statement: AI review is not human approval. Future source evidence requires safe source review. Current PR does not review or select a driver.

bestOfN.selectedCandidate: Candidate B - create staleness and revalidation policy while keeping committed evidence not_reviewed.
bestOfN.reasonSelected: It prevents future stale source evidence from supporting driver selection without selecting or installing a driver now.
rejectedAlternatives: Candidate A allows raw audit/advisory snippets for reviewer convenience, but raw payloads cause leakage, token bloat, and unsafe evidence.
Candidate C skips staleness and relies on manual checks, leaving target commit and package version mismatch risk.

testCoverageEvidence.changedArea: DB driver source evidence staleness validator, expiry windows, invalidation triggers, future fresh fixture, docs, and .codex evidence.
testCoverageEvidence.testCommand: corepack pnpm test and npm test cover source evidence staleness with existing repository tests.
coverage: Default committed evidence remains policy_ready and not_reviewed.
Candidate staleness remains not_reviewed for pg and postgres.
Future fresh fixture is accepted only through test-only validator.

Expired timestamps, future timestamps, target/base/PR/branch/package/source mismatches, raw output, private URLs, DB connection strings, wallet addresses, token-like values, and unsafe safe-summary wording are rejected.

solvabilityConstraints: No DB driver dependency may be added.
No package.json or pnpm-lock change may be introduced.
No real DB connection, migration apply, live DB integration test, provider SDK apply, production deployment, or readiness claim may be introduced.
Committed machine evidence must remain not_ready, not_selected, blocked, and unapproved.
Future owner approval and final approval gate remain separate work.
storageOracle: Committed advisory envelope evidence remains not_reviewed, driverChoiceStatus not_selected, selectedDriver null, candidateDrivers pg/postgres, knownBlockers null, rawOutputPolicyStatus safe_summary_only, owner approval not_approved, final gate blocked, and all permission flags false.
releaseGateOracle: GitHub typescript/contracts/quality-gate required checks on the current PR head plus evidence:ci and quality:self-protection.
summary: Adds DB driver source evidence staleness policy while keeping committed evidence policy_ready and not_reviewed without selecting or installing a driver.
